description_long: This traffic light installation features a robust, dark-colored metal pole with a rectangular base for stability. Attached to the pole are several control boxes, one of which is a prominent mint-green color with visible access panels and labels. The unit includes a blue circular pedestrian and bicycle sign, as well as a dual signal light with red and green illuminated sections, accompanied by signage with Asian script. Multiple cables and conduits are neatly routed along the frame, connecting the control boxes and signal lights. The structure is designed for urban pedestrian crossings, with clear visual indicators for both vehicles and foot traffic. The overall build is industrial and weather-resistant, suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description: A pole-mounted traffic light with control boxes, pedestrian/bicycle signage, and dual signal lights, designed for urban crossings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main pole, control boxes, blue pedestrian/bicycle sign, and the dual signal lights with visible wiring.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the control box and the arrangement of cables along the frame.
description_view_2: View showing the illuminated signal lights, signage with Asian script, and the pedestrian/bicycle sign clearly.
description_view_3: Rear-side view focusing on the back of the control box and the cable management, with less visibility of the signal lights.
